message:
docs: tighten two oversells found in the live-site sweep

- index.mdx hero: drop "edge runtimes" — we don't actually target
  or test edge runtimes (Cloudflare Workers etc.). Replace the line
  with the four surfaces we do ship: browser, Node native, CLI,
  pre-commit, GitHub Action. Same description for SEO.

- cli.mdx: drop the colloquial "Just Works" capitalisation.
  Replace with what literally happens: "the other reuses the same
  cache on the same machine — no re-download."

Other red-flag word sweeps came back clean. "X× faster" claims
all name the alternative + hardware. The "Personal learning
project" Aside on the index is doing the right tone-setting work
and the cold-start section explicitly disclaims a speedup we
haven't measured.

files.patch:
@@ -131,4 +131,4 @@ jobs:
 | Browser app / front-end | Library (`import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift/browser"`) |
 | Streaming AI proxy | Library — streaming `detect()` / `redact()` only available via the JS API |
 
-The CLI and lib share the same model cache, native binaries, and fallback logic. Whichever you reach for first, the other Just Works on the same machine without re-download.
+The CLI and lib share the same model cache, native binaries, and fallback logic. Whichever you reach for first, the other reuses the same cache on the same machine — no re-download.

@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
 ---
 title: textsift
-description: Browser-native PII detection and redaction using openai/privacy-filter.
+description: Local-first PII detection and redaction. Runs openai/privacy-filter on-device in the browser, Node, and a CLI. Same engine, four surfaces.
 template: splash
 hero:
-  tagline: OpenAI's privacy-filter model running locally in the browser, Node, and edge runtimes.
+  tagline: openai/privacy-filter running locally — browser (WebGPU), Node native (Metal/Vulkan/Dawn), CLI, pre-commit hook, and GitHub Action.
   actions:
     - text: Quickstart
       link: /textsift/quickstart/
